source.title: Endogenous electric fields may guide neocortical network activity

confidence_rationale: Direct demonstration that weak sinusoidal and naturalistic endogenous-amplitude fields enhance and entrain physiological network activity in neocortical slices, with a susceptibility threshold within in vivo field strengths. The feedback-loop experiment -- applying fields that are themselves functions of real-time network activity -- is the architectural analogue of MECH-270's claim that the field is simultaneously generated by the coherent population and read by that same population. Confidence discounted because the slice is neocortex (not hippocampus), because the network regime studied is the slow oscillation (not SWR), and because the paper characterizes whole-network entrainment rather than the stream-local coherence readout MECH-270 needs.
failure_signatures: If endogenous-amplitude fields could not measurably entrain physiological network activity under feedback conditions, MECH-270's proposal that field coherence is both generated by and read by coherent ensembles would lack the closed-loop empirical foundation it needs.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Weak sinusoidal and naturalistic electric fields, at amplitudes matching measured in vivo endogenous field strengths, enhance and entrain neocortical network activity during the slow oscillation in vitro. Feedback fields driven by real-time network activity provide direct evidence of a closed loop between neuronal spiking and the fields that activity generates.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-270's architecture requires not just that fields influence firing (Anastassiou 2011), but that the field is both generated by the coherent population and read by it -- a closed loop in which field coherence is an observable of population coherence and a cause of subsequent coordinated spiking. Frohlich & McCormick's feedback-field experiment is the cleanest demonstration of that closed loop in mammalian cortex. That is the architectural shape MECH-270 needs, even though the regime (neocortical slow oscillation) is not the hippocampal SWR regime the claim is ultimately about.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Neocortex not hippocampus; slow oscillation not SWR; the paper characterizes whole-network entrainment, not the stream-local coherence readout MECH-270 specifies. The closed-loop architectural lesson transfers, but the amplitude and population-coherence thresholds in CA1/CA3 during SWR would need their own measurements before MECH-270 could be considered directly supported. This is scaffolding for the claim, not a direct test.
mapping.source_context: Ferret neocortical slice in vitro; 12-electrode spatial field application; recording during spontaneous slow-oscillation UP/DOWN states; real-time feedback protocol in which applied field was computed from the ongoing LFP.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron paper, McCormick is a senior authority on cortical dynamics. Mapping fidelity discounted because the regime is neocortical slow oscillation not hippocampal SWR, and because the paper characterizes whole-network entrainment without decomposing by subpopulation coherence. Transfer risk moderate: the closed-loop field-feedback logic is what MECH-270 architecturally needs, but the quantitative regime for hippocampal ephaptic readout is not established by this paper.
